prose.en: SSH-agent and similar key caching applications manage private keys stored on workstations and servers. Specifically, when an SSH-agent launches, it requests a user’s password to unlock the user’s private key. Subsequent access to remote systems is then performed by the SSH-agent and does not require the user to re-enter their password. Screen locks and expiring key caches can be used to ensure that a user’s private key is not left unlocked for a long period.
